dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Built CLI checks passed for the main happy/unhappy path (`go build ./cmd/furrow`; valid `--json` => exit 0, invalid => exit 3), but schema-invalid fixtures such as `deliverables[].file_ownership: wrong`, `context_pointers[].symbols: wrong`, `constraints: [1]`, and `source_todos: [Bad_Pattern]` were accepted as valid, so the implementation does not satisfy the full-schema-validation acceptance criterion.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/validate_definition_test.go` covers the branching entrypoints and helpers (`runValidateDefinition`, `validateDefinition`, `runValidate`, `nonEmptyString`, `validModeMissing`, `hasPlaceholderText`) and includes the required happy-path and core invalid-path cases; `go test ./...` passes.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: The interface shape is mostly correct (`internal/cli/app.go` routes `validate`, shared dispatcher lives in `internal/cli/validate.go`, JSON envelope uses `BlockerEnvelope`, taxonomy panic is enforced in `internal/cli/blocker_envelope.go`), but the validator does not implement the schema contract in `schemas/definition.schema.json` fully, so AC 3 is not met.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: For D1 itself, the modified files match the row’s declared `file_ownership` in `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/definition.yaml`, including the explicitly justified shared-surface files `internal/cli/validate.go` and `internal/cli/app.go`.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: The Go code is formatted and test-clean (`go test ./...` passes), the shell shim is thin with no business logic, and emitted codes are taxonomy-backed with test-time panic protection for unregistered codes.
